narratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
narratives.reasoning: The text actively praises Russia by highlighting its diplomatic gesture of sending a higher-ranking official to welcome PM Modi compared to China's President, framing it as a sign of Russia prioritizing India, which is a positive evaluation of Russia's foreign policy actions.

narratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता। बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
narratives.reasoning: The text promotes India's stance that a solution cannot be found through military means alone, implying criticism of the Western-led approach which has heavily relied on military support for Ukraine. By stating that diplomacy and dialogue are the only solution, it indirectly discredits the Western diplomatic and military strategy in the conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
subnarratives.reasoning: The text actively promotes the idea that Russia is valued and prioritized by a major global power (India), implying Russia's strong and preferential international relationships, which aligns with the subnarrative of Russia having international support.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: शीत युद्ध के समय से ही भारत-रूस के संबंध अच्छे हैं, साथ ही रूस भारत को सबसे अधिक हथियार सप्लाई करने वाले देशों में भी शामिल है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement praises Russia's long-standing, positive strategic partnership with India and its role as a key defense supplier, which supports the parent narrative of praising Russia but does not fit any of the more specific subnarrative definitions provided (e.g., it's not solely about military might, Putin, peace, or support for the invasion).

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly discredits the Western approach to the Ukraine war, which heavily involves military support for Ukraine, by stating that a solution cannot be found through military means alone. This undermines the efficacy of the West's primary strategy and promotes the idea that their chosen path (military support) is ineffective, aligning with the subnarrative that diplomacy (as an alternative) is being dismissed or that the current Western-led approach is futile.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement positions India and the 'Global South' as the proponents of the correct, peaceful diplomatic approach (dialogue and diplomacy), in implicit contrast to the West, which is portrayed as favoring the battlefield. This supports the parent narrative of discrediting Western diplomacy by suggesting the West's stance is not aligned with the wiser, more peaceful consensus of the Global South.
